bird.name: New Zealand fernbird
bird.summary: The New Zealand fernbird or simply fernbird (Poodytes punctatus) is an insectivorous bird endemic to New Zealand. In the Māori language, it…

relations.subject: New Zealand fernbird
relations.evidence: In the 19th century, Walter Buller described it as "one of our most common" birds, but it has been adversely affected by the subsequent widespread destruction of its natural wetland habitat following European settlement and is now rare. The New Zealand fernbird lives mainly in wet shrubby environments with swamps, peat bogs or ponds, plantation areas, temperate shrublands. It occupies an extremely wide area of occurrence at 657,000 km². Field studies of the South Island fernbird showed they preferred shrubby habitats with low dense ground vegetation near an estuary margin. The New Zealand fernbird is insectivorous
relations.object_summary: 海湾、泻湖、盐沼与河口组成的复合湿地。

relations.object_summary: 淡水与海水交汇形成的高生产力边缘生态系统。

relations.subject: New Zealand fernbird
relations.evidence: |last8=O'Donnell |first8=Colin |last9=Sagar |first9=Paul |last10=Scofield |first10=Richard Paul |last11=Taylor |first11=Graeme Arthur |last12=Michel |first12=Pascale |date=2021-12-01 |title=Conservation status of birds in Aotearoa New Zealand, 2021 |url=https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q124308704 |journal=New Zealand Threat Classification Series |language=English}} the subspecies have the following threat status: * Poodytes punctatus vealeae – North Island – Declining * Poodytes punctatus punctatus – South Island – Declining * Poodytes punctatus stewartianus – Stewart Island – Nationally Vulnerable * Poodytes punctatus wilsoni – Codfish Island | Whenua Hou – Naturally Uncommon * Poodytes punctatus caudatus – Snares Island – Naturally Uncommon New Zealand fernbird populations are affected by wetland drainage and rat predation. Positive short-term affects on fernbird breeding, and no negative affects, were documented in a 2021 study to control introduced mammalian predators
relations.object_summary: 湿地被排干转为农业或建设用地。
